title: Height of Binary Tree

content.problemStatement:
Given a connected binary tree with **N** nodes, find the height of it. (**Note:** The height of a binary tree is the number of edges between the tree's root and its furthest leaf.)

For example, the following binary tree has height `2`:



Hint: While traversing the Tree, you will have to store the height of children and use that to calculate the height of parent.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN — the number of nodes in the binary tree.
Next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain three space separated characters pi,ci,Rip_i, c_i, R_ipi​,ci​,Ri​, describing the tree with edge informations - pip_ipi​ is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, cic_ici​ is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi​ denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if cic_ici​ is left child of pip_ipi​, else `R`.


Output Format
Output on the single line, the height of the given binary tree.
constraints: The given edges always form a valid binary tree with root 1
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N — the number of nodes in the binary tree (nodes are numbered from 1 to N, root is always node 1). The next N‑1 lines each contain three space‑separated values: pi ci Ri, where pi is the parent node, ci is the child node and Ri is either 'L' (ci is the left child of pi) or 'R' (ci is the right child of pi).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the height of the given binary tree, defined as the number of edges on the longest root‑to‑leaf path.

solution.approach: The tree is given as parent‑child relations with left/right information. We store for each node its left and right child (0 if absent). The height of a node is max(height(left), height(right)) + 1, where the height of a null child is -1 so that a leaf gets height 0. Starting from the root (node 1) we compute the height recursively. This runs in O(N) per test case because each node is visited once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the left and right child arrays and recursion stack.
